title: Basic Science
description: Question batch for the Chemistry Basics domain.

questions.question: What is the smallest unit of an element?
questions.explanation: An atom is the basic unit of an element.

questions.question: What is a molecule?
answers: Two or more atoms bonded together
questions.explanation: Molecules are groups of bonded atoms.

questions.question: Which particle has a positive charge?
questions.explanation: Protons carry positive charge.

questions.question: Which particle has a negative charge?
questions.explanation: Electrons carry negative charge.

questions.question: What is the center of an atom called?
questions.explanation: The nucleus is the atom's center.

questions.question: What does H2O represent?
questions.explanation: H2O is the formula for water.

questions.question: What is pH used to measure?
questions.explanation: pH measures how acidic or basic a substance is.

questions.question: Which state of matter has a fixed shape?
questions.explanation: Solids keep a fixed shape.

questions.question: Which state of matter fills its container fully?
questions.explanation: Gases expand to fill their container.

questions.question: What is an element?
answers: A pure substance made of one type of atom
questions.explanation: An element contains only one kind of atom.

questions.question: What is a compound?
answers: Two or more elements chemically bonded
questions.explanation: Compounds contain elements bonded chemically.

questions.question: What is a mixture?
questions.explanation: Mixtures combine substances physically, not chemically.

questions.question: What is an ion?
answers: An atom or molecule with charge
questions.explanation: Ions have gained or lost electrons.

questions.question: What type of bond involves shared electrons?
questions.explanation: Covalent bonds share electrons.

questions.question: What type of bond forms from electron transfer?
questions.explanation: Ionic bonds form after electron transfer creates ions.

questions.question: What happens during evaporation?
questions.explanation: Evaporation is liquid turning into gas.

questions.question: What happens during condensation?
questions.explanation: Condensation is gas turning into liquid.

questions.question: What does a catalyst do?
answers: Speeds a reaction without being used up
questions.explanation: Catalysts speed reactions and remain largely unchanged.

questions.question: Which gas do humans need to breathe?
questions.explanation: Humans need oxygen for respiration.

questions.question: What is rust mainly?
questions.explanation: Rust forms when iron oxidizes.

questions.question: Why does salt dissolve in water?
questions.explanation: Polar water molecules surround and separate ions.

questions.question: What does conservation of mass mean in chemistry?
answers: Mass stays constant in a closed system
questions.explanation: Matter is rearranged, not destroyed, in ordinary reactions.

questions.question: Why do acids and bases react?
questions.explanation: Acids and bases can react to form water and salts.

questions.question: Why does heating often speed reactions?
questions.explanation: Higher temperature increases collision energy and frequency.

questions.question: What makes diamond and graphite different?
questions.explanation: Both are carbon, but with different structures.

questions.question: Why is water called a universal solvent?
questions.explanation: Water dissolves many materials because of its polarity.

questions.question: What is an exothermic reaction?
answers: A reaction that absorbs all light
questions.explanation: Exothermic reactions release energy as heat.

questions.question: Why are noble gases less reactive?
answers: Their outer electron shells are full
questions.explanation: Full outer shells make noble gases stable.

questions.question: What is the periodic table organized by?
questions.explanation: Elements are ordered mainly by atomic number.

questions.question: Why is carbon central in organic chemistry?
questions.explanation: Carbon can form diverse stable structures and chains.
